name: greedy-toasting-metcalfe
description: Parallel issue work on 15 Kapow issues

members.name: posthog-wiring
members.prompt:
## Task
You are working on beads issue **kapow-d1i.6: PostHog provider wiring** (Task #1).

Wire PostHogProvider from posthog-js/react into main.tsx provider hierarchy: StrictMode > UrqlProvider > AppProviders > PostHogProvider > ErrorBoundary > BrowserRouter > App. This is the final assembly step that activates the PostHog integration.

**Acceptance criteria**: PostHogProvider wraps the app; provider hierarchy correct; pnpm build && pnpm lint pass with zero errors.

## WORKTREE SETUP — RUN FIRST
Before doing anything else, create and enter an isolated worktree:
```bash
REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
BRANCH_NAME="worktree/posthog-wiring"
WORKTREE_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/.claude/worktrees/$BRANCH_NAME"

if [ -d "$WORKTREE_DIR" ]; then
  echo "Worktree already exists, reusing"
else
  git worktree add "$WORKTREE_DIR" -b "$BRANCH_NAME"
fi

cd "$WORKTREE_DIR"
```
Verify you are in the right place:
```bash
echo "CWD: $(pwd)"
echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)"
echo "$(pwd)" | grep -q '\.claude/worktrees/' && echo "WORKTREE VERIFIED" || echo "FATAL: NOT in a worktree"
```
If verification fails, STOP and report the error to the team lead.

ALL subsequent commands must run from this worktree directory.
NEVER cd back to the main repo directory. Commit and push ONLY on your worktree branch — never touch main.

## Quality Gate (must pass before reporting done)
```bash
cd web && pnpm install && pnpm build && pnpm lint && pnpm test
```

## When Done
1. Commit your changes with a descriptive message
2. Push your branch: `git push -u origin HEAD`
3. Report completion to team lead with quality gate output
4. Mark Task #1 as completed via TaskUpdate

Follow all conventions in CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md.

members.name: deadline-search-fields
members.prompt:
## Task
You are working on beads issue **kapow-t77.10: Search fields and GraphQL for deadlines** (Task #2).

Expose deadline data through the search API and GraphQL schema.

**Specific changes needed:**

api/internal/search/datasource.go:
- Add SourceBigFuture data source (id=bigfuture, dataset=deadlines)

api/internal/search/registry.go — new search fields:
- has_early_decision (SingleEnumField, searchable, Yes/No)
- has_early_action (SingleEnumField, searchable, Yes/No)
- has_rolling_admissions (SingleEnumField, searchable, Yes/No)
- application_fee (NumericRangeField, searchable, USD)
- Date fields (deadlines, notifications) as output-only/exportable initially

api/graph/schema/search.graphql — extend InstitutionAdmissions:
- regularDecisionDeadline, earlyDecisionDeadline, earlyActionDeadline: String
- regularNotificationDate: String
- hasEarlyDecision, hasEarlyAction, hasRollingAdmissions: Boolean
- applicationFee: Int, feeWaiver: Boolean

api/graph/resolver/search_data_extract.go:
- Wire deadline fields from data->'deadlines'->'plans'->... JSONB paths

Then: make api-generate && make web-schema-sync

**Acceptance**: make test-standard passes, new fields appear in search metadata endpoint.

## WORKTREE SETUP — RUN FIRST
Before doing anything else, create and enter an isolated worktree:
```bash
REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
BRANCH_NAME="worktree/deadline-search-fields"
WORKTREE_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/.claude/worktrees/$BRANCH_NAME"

if [ -d "$WORKTREE_DIR" ]; then
  echo "Worktree already exists, reusing"
else
  git worktree add "$WORKTREE_DIR" -b "$BRANCH_NAME"
fi

cd "$WORKTREE_DIR"
```
Verify you are in the right place:
```bash
echo "CWD: $(pwd)"
echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)"
echo "$(pwd)" | grep -q '\.claude/worktrees/' && echo "WORKTREE VERIFIED" || echo "FATAL: NOT in a worktree"
```
If verification fails, STOP and report the error to the team lead.

ALL subsequent commands must run from this worktree directory.
NEVER cd back to the main repo directory. Commit and push ONLY on your worktree branch — never touch main.

## Quality Gate (must pass before reporting done)
```bash
make api-generate && make web-schema-sync && make test-standard
```

## When Done
1. Commit your changes with a descriptive message
2. Push your branch: `git push -u origin HEAD`
3. Report completion to team lead with quality gate output
4. Mark Task #2 as completed via TaskUpdate

Follow all conventions in CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md.

members.name: deadline-overrides
members.prompt:
## Task
You are working on beads issue **kapow-t77.9: Manual deadline overrides file** (Task #3).

Create tools/kapow/data/deadline_overrides.json with manual corrections and REA classifications.

Keyed by unit_id, contains partial DeadlineData merged with highest priority over Tier 1/2 data. Initially populated with ~20 REA schools: Harvard, Yale, Stanford, Princeton, Notre Dame, Georgetown, Boston College, etc.

members.name: playwright-scraper
members.prompt:
## Task
You are working on beads issue **kapow-t77.5: Playwright scraper infrastructure** (Task #4).

Add Playwright-based page rendering for Tier 1 institution site scraping.

**Specific changes:**

Add playwright-go dependency to tools/kapow/go.mod.

internal/deadlines/scraper.go:
- Scraper struct managing a Playwright browser instance
- NewScraper(ctx, opts) — launch headless Chromium, ScraperOpts: Concurrency, Timeout, UserAgent
- RenderPage(ctx, url) → RenderedPage{HTML, Text, URL, StatusCode, FetchedAt} — navigate, wait for network idle, extract text via page.InnerText(body)
- Close() — shutdown browser
- Semaphore-based concurrency control (default 2 tabs)

internal/deadlines/urlresolver.go:
- ResolveAdmissionsURL(ctx, client, baseURL) — try common paths (/admissions, /apply, /admission, /admissions/deadlines, /apply/dates-deadlines), return first 200
- LoadInstitutionURLs(ctx, pool) — query nces.school_url from dataset_merged
- Manual overrides from tools/kapow/data/admissions_urls.json

**Acceptance**: can render a few known institution admissions pages and extract readable text.

## WORKTREE SETUP — RUN FIRST
Before doing anything else, create and enter an isolated worktree:
```bash
REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
BRANCH_NAME="worktree/playwright-scraper"
WORKTREE_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/.claude/worktrees/$BRANCH_NAME"

if [ -d "$WORKTREE_DIR" ]; then
  echo "Worktree already exists, reusing"
else
  git worktree add "$WORKTREE_DIR" -b "$BRANCH_NAME"
fi

cd "$WORKTREE_DIR"
```
Verify you are in the right place:
```bash

echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)"
echo "$(pwd)" | grep -q '\.claude/worktrees/' && echo "WORKTREE VERIFIED" || echo "FATAL: NOT in a worktree"
```
If verification fails, STOP and report the error to the team lead.

ALL subsequent commands must run from this worktree directory.
NEVER cd back to the main repo directory. Commit and push ONLY on your worktree branch — never touch main.

## Quality Gate (must pass before reporting done)
```bash

```

## When Done
1. Commit your changes with a descriptive message

3. Report completion to team lead with quality gate output

members.name: loading-states
members.prompt:
## Task
You are working on beads issue **kapow-5ln: Data source loading states** (Task #5).

Expose fetching and error state from the data-source hook and update consumers accordingly. The current hook returns only a map, so pages like About cannot distinguish loading from failure and can show an infinite spinner.

**Acceptance criteria**: The data-source hook exposes loading and error state. About renders clear loading, error, and empty states. Metadata-dependent UI no longer treats fetch failure as perpetual loading. Tests cover all states.

members.name: graphql-guardrails
members.prompt:
## Task
You are working on beads issue **kapow-3a4: GraphQL server guardrails** (Task #6).

The GraphQL server has rate limiting, but lacks request-body limits, query complexity limits, or depth limits. For a public query surface, that leaves avoidable denial-of-service and runaway-query risk.

**Acceptance criteria**: GraphQL requests are bounded by explicit server-side guardrails appropriate to the stack, such as request body size limits and gqlgen complexity or depth controls. Limits are documented and test-covered. Legitimate search traffic continues to work within the configured thresholds.

## WORKTREE SETUP — RUN FIRST
Before doing anything else, create and enter an isolated worktree:
```bash
REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
BRANCH_NAME="worktree/graphql-guardrails"
WORKTREE_DIR="$REPO_ROOT/.claude/worktrees/$BRANCH_NAME"

if [ -d "$WORKTREE_DIR" ]; then
  echo "Worktree already exists, reusing"
else
  git worktree add "$WORKTREE_DIR" -b "$BRANCH_NAME"
fi

cd "$WORKTREE_DIR"
```
Verify you are in the right place:
```bash
echo "CWD: $(pwd)"
echo "Branch: $(git branch --show-current)"
echo "$(pwd)" | grep -q '\.claude/worktrees/' && echo "WORKTREE VERIFIED" || echo "FATAL: NOT in a worktree"
```
If verification fails, STOP and report the error to the team lead.

ALL subsequent commands must run from this worktree directory.
NEVER cd back to the main repo directory. Commit and push ONLY on your worktree branch — never touch main.

## Quality Gate (must pass before reporting done)
```bash
make api-test-unit && make api-lint
```

## When Done
1. Commit your changes with a descriptive message
2. Push your branch: `git push -u origin HEAD`
3. Report completion to team lead with quality gate output
4. Mark Task #6 as completed via TaskUpdate

Follow all conventions in CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md.

members.name: match-reporting
members.prompt:
## Task
You are working on beads issue **kapow-j2l.11: Match reporting + fuzzy suggestions** (Task #7).

After running all platform extractors, produce a structured match report.

Output: {cacheDir}/admissions/match-report-{YYYY-MM-DD}.json

Report contents:
- Per-platform stats: total scraped, matched, unmatched, match rate %
- Overall stats: total institutions updated, total platforms per institution
- Unmatched entries: institution name, platform, top 3 fuzzy candidates with Jaro-Winkler scores (using namematch.FuzzyMatcher)
- Summary logged to slog at info level

This report directly informs alias additions — unmatched names with high fuzzy scores are candidates for new aliases.

Tests: Report generation with mock match data. Verify JSON structure.

## WORKTREE SETUP — RUN FIRST
Before doing anything else, create and enter an isolated worktree:
```bash
REPO_ROOT="$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)"
```

members.name: zip-lookup-fix
members.prompt:
## Task
You are working on beads issue **kapow-e5n: ZIP lookup single-submit flow** (Task #8).

Fix the ZIP-based location flow so one submit resolves location reliably. Today ZIP resolution is query-timing dependent, which can force extra clicks and produce a confusing looking-up state.

**Acceptance criteria**: Entering a valid ZIP and clicking Go resolves location in one submit. Users do not need to submit twice. Loading, invalid-format, and not-found states are distinct. Tests cover success, pending, invalid, and missing ZIP behavior.

## WORKTREE SETUP — RUN FIRST
